text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 21 September 2025 

Dr Helen Morris  
Riverside Health Centre  
48 Brook Street, London W1K 5DL  

Dear Dr Helen Morris,  

Daniel Cooper, DOB: 22-07-1984, Hospital No: U784231 NHS No. 5432198765  
19 Willow Grove, London, SE15 3QT 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Remote history of mild traumatic brain injury. Ongoing follow‑up for seizures. No current alcohol. Non-smoker. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily. Vitamin D3 1000 IU once daily. Omeprazole 20 mg once daily. 

I reviewed him in clinic today, accompanied by his partner. He reports good day-to-day function, working part-time and exercising regularly. Sleep and mood are stable. No headaches or focal neurological complaints. Neurological examination today was unremarkable, including normal gait and coordination. 

Seizures: He and his partner keep a shared electronic log. There has been no occurrence of events suggestive of seizures since his last review twelve months ago, and he describes no episodes of loss of awareness, convulsions, or auras. 

Plan: 
- Continue current antiseizure medication without change.  
- Routine bloods (U&E, LFTs, FBC, vitamin D) via GP within the next 3 months as part of ongoing monitoring.  
- Reinforced general safety advice and adherence.  
- Follow-up in 12 months, sooner if any concerning events occur. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: There has been no occurrence of events suggestive of seizures since his last review twelve months ago

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "There has been no occurrence of events suggestive of seizures since his last review twelve months ago," and also notes no episodes of loss of awareness, convulsions, or auras. The clinic date is 21 September 2025, and the last review was twelve months prior, fulfilling the ≥6-month seizure-free criterion. Therefore, the patient is seizure free for 12 months.

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 12 month) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
